context: Cloud-API frontier-judge re-eval (task #122 round 7) post iter-275 v8 engine promotion + iter-336 PHI source-scan lock-in. Companion to round-6 local-LLM run (composite 7.56 on n=5/6 Ollama Q4_K_M judges). Round-7 uses xAI Grok 4.1 fast-reasoning, Mistral Large, DeepSeek-Chat, Perplexity Sonar Pro, NVIDIA Nemotron Ultra 253B — frontier-tier reasoning models for higher-quality signal.

blocking_gaps_for_10_10.gap: Relies on synthetic Synthea patients and small 139-pair cohort; lacks diverse real-world clinical validation or peer review beyond single advisor.
Claims 1371 tests and CI green but provides no coverage percentages, logs, or badges in package.
Novel Q16.16 BitNet impressive but lacks details on training pipeline, hyperparameters, or cross-validation beyond held-out fold.

strengths: Exceptional JUDGES.md with 60-second summary, 5-minute audit script, and head-to-head differentiation table.
Core reproducibility primitive with bit-identical Q16.16 replay, Ed25519 audit chain, and single-command gates.
Comprehensive FHIR R4 coverage (18 resources), Apache-2.0 patent grant, and clinician advisor with verifiable NPI.

blocking_gaps_for_10_10.gap: Lacks peer-reviewed publication or preprint demonstrating novel CS contributions beyond BitNet adaptation.
No documented IRB review or real-world clinical validation beyond synthetic data and advisor attestation.
Hero section lacks a 5-second video thumbnail or animated GIF to immediately convey the bit-identical replay concept.
No high-fidelity Figma/Adobe XD source files provided for design system consistency audit.

strengths: Bit-identical Q16.16 fixed-point reproducibility across architectures (x86_64/ARM64/RISC-V/Pi Zero/A100) is a standout innovation with clear FDA defensibility.
21 CFR Part 11 audit chain with Ed25519 signing and SHA-256 predecessor hashing is production-grade and exceeds typical hackathon submissions.
Comprehensive test coverage (1371 tests) with CI reproducibility manifest checks demonstrates exceptional engineering rigor.
Apache-2.0 license with explicit patent grant and no buried clauses sets a gold standard for open-source ecosystem fit.
JUDGES.md is a masterclass in judge-focused documentation, providing 5-second, 5-minute, and 1-hour audit paths with clear evidence trails.

blocking_gaps_for_10_10.gap: No novel CS contribution beyond Q16.16 fixed-point; the architecture is a well-known pipeline assembly.
The core differentiator (bit-identical replay) is narrow; Epic Sense and Hippocratic AI have broader clinical deployment and FDA clearance.
Only one clinical advisor (Dr. Afonicheva) and a 139-pair cohort; lacks multi-institution validation or real-world EHR integration.
No evidence of external contributions or community adoption; contribution path is declared but untested.
Q16.16 replay is demonstrated but not validated against a third-party auditor or across diverse hardware beyond the team's own tests.
Demo is text-only in JUDGES.md; no interactive live demo URL or video walkthrough provided in the submission package.
FDA Q-Sub draft is mentioned but not included in the submission; README is thorough but lacks a quick-start for non-technical judges.
No gap; license is clear and complete.
1371 tests are impressive but no coverage percentage or mutation testing evidence is provided.
Dashboard is described but not rendered; visual hierarchy and brand consistency cannot be fully assessed without a live view.

strengths: Bit-identical Q16.16 fixed-point replay across architectures is a genuine safety primitive that no existing clinical AI product offers.
100% recall on contraindicated drug pairs (44/44) with zero false positives on a negative-control cohort demonstrates strong clinical precision.
Apache-2.0 license with explicit patent grant and 21 CFR Part 11 audit chain implementation shows serious regulatory awareness.
1371 passing tests, CI reproducibility manifest checks, and 30 audit-chain unit tests indicate engineering rigor.
Six-model US-based LLM consensus with abstention gate addresses hallucination and data residency concerns.
